text.tspan: 1.2. Shape runs in the order provided by BiDi algorithm.
Shaping failed (some returned glyphs have index "0").
Reshape entire run instead of missing glyphs, otherwise
we can get inconsistent result like this:
2.1. Locate valid break positions (using ICU break iterator, or simplified fallback break iterator).

text.tspan: 2.2. Reorder clusters in the logical order.

ICU BiDi context (but not BiDi algorithm results!) can
Fully identical runs can be reused without reshaping.
3.1. Locate word bounds (using ICU break iterator).
3.1.1. For Arabic runs additionally look for kashida justification points in each word.
Shape single code point (U+0640) run into kashida cluster:
3.2.1. If shaped successfully, insert as many kashida clusters as possible.
3.2.2. Elongate (change advance) or insert spaces in the rest of word bounds.

text.tspan: Some languages don't use spaces between the words.
Points on the boundaries of different direction runs correspond to multiple visual positions.
New LTR text will be inserted here.
Most UI toolkits draw both all the time.
To detect which one is more relevant use ether last entered text direction or selected keyboard layout.
Shortcut to manually switch primary cursor may be useful in case both of cursors don't fit to screen simultaneously.

text.tspan: 1.1. Split the string into BiDi and Script runs, then combine attribute/bidi and script runs into monotone runs.
BiDi is designed only for plain text and can not properly process text with higher order structure (paths, breadcrumbs, URLs, e-mail addresses, math formulas, dates, reg.exps., source code, etc)
Rendering structured text can be done either by tokenizing it and rendering each part individually or by injecting Unicode control chars.
2.4. Rerun BiDi and shaping on each line.
PRI #185: Extension of UBA for improved display of URL/IRIs

g.aria-label: Test Ξεσκεπάζω رحيم رحيم exténd itşţșț

text.tspan: "HarfBuzz is a text-shaping engine. If you give HarfBuzz a font and a string containing a sequence of Unicode code points,
HarfBuzz selects and positions the corresponding glyphs from the font, applying all of the necessary layout rules and font
features. HarfBuzz then returns the string to you in the form that is correctly arranged for the language and writing system."
Text direction is detected based on first encountered code point (AUTO), current locale (LOCALE) or specified by user. Base direction is used to initialize BiDi iterator .
Shaping and font fallback (Attributed String Variant)
* in case of plain (non attributed) string font/language and features are specified for entire string.
Character - ambiguous term, can mean anything
Code unit - atomic unit of the encodeing (16-bits in case of UTF-16), represent a full code point, or part of a code point
Code point - atomic unit of the Unicode standard (independent of encoding)
Glyph - an image, atomic element of the font (font specific, doesn't have any meaning outside of the font)
Grapheme cluster - atomic unit of a writing system
HarfBuzz returns font specific glyph indicies,
offsets, advanced and character ranges that
Ascent and descent of cluster is calculated based
on the font ascent/descent and glyph offsets.
2.3.2. Break line at to last fitting valid break.
Not implemented in the gdtl module.
